name: Queen’s Gambit
description: 1.d4 d5 2.c4 — Declined, Slav and Accepted structures.
source: curated repertoire (scripts/repertoires/queens-gambit.pgn) + lichess explorer, ratings 1400/1600/1800, speeds blitz/rapid

children.comment: The rook hits c7 before Black's bishop reaches b7 comfortably.
The Tartakower trade: now the c-file and the d5 hanging pawn define the game.
Keep the queenside pressure; c7 needs a defender for the rest of the game.
Lasker's freeing idea: trade pieces, keep the draw in sight.
Prepare e4 and Rd1: the centre comes with tempo while Black is still unpacking.
No rush: 3.e4 allows counterplay, and the c4 pawn cannot be held anyway (3...b5?! 4.a4).
Ready for e4: the whole line exists for this central break.
Stops ...b5: Black cannot keep the extra pawn, and ...Bf5 costs the b7 pawn its defender.
The Semi-Slav: a solid Meran setup without memorising forcing lines.
Hold the extra pawn, return it for development only on your terms.
Rules out every ...Bb4+ trick before developing.
The only move worth knowing: 4.e3?? Bb4+ 5.Bd2 dxe3! is the Lasker Trap and it is losing.
Simplify: two bishops and the better pawns in every ending.

variations.name: Declined main line
variations.intro: The classical battleground: Bg5, e3, and long-term pressure on d5. Against the Tartakower ...b6, trade into the structure and squeeze.

variations.name: Accepted
variations.intro: 2...dxc4 gives up the centre to win a tempo fight it cannot win. Recover the pawn calmly and enjoy the better development.

variations.name: Slav
variations.intro: The Slav keeps the c8 bishop free - so make it commit. The main line wins the tempo battle around the c4 pawn and builds the e4 centre.

variations.name: Albin and Chigorin
variations.intro: The romantic defences. Against the Albin, know one move: 4.Nf3 - never 4.e3??, which walks into the Lasker Trap. Against the Chigorin, trade into the bishop-pair endgame.

variations.name: Cambridge Springs Defence — main line with 7.Nd2!
